interfaces.name: GameEngine
interfaces.description: Core Sudoku logic: board representation, move validation, puzzle loading, undo/redo handling
interfaces.contract: Exports initPuzzle(difficulty), getBoard(), makeMove(cell,row,col,value), undo(), redo(), isSolved()

interfaces.name: UIRenderer
interfaces.description: DOM rendering and event handling for the grid, controls, timer, and overlays
interfaces.contract: Exports renderBoard(board), highlightCell(row,col), showCompletion(data), updateTimer(seconds), updateMistakes(count)

interfaces.name: TimerService
interfaces.description: Accurate game timer with pause/resume capabilities

dataContracts.notes: Curated list of valid puzzles for each difficulty level
Current numbers, fixed flags, and validity status
Tracks user moves for undo/redo; each entry includes cell coordinates, previous value, new value

scalability.expectedLoad: One browser instance per user; max concurrent sessions limited by client hardware
bottlenecks: DOM reflow on each cell update
mitigations: Use lightweight data structures and avoid full board redraw
Limit timer resolution to 1 s and pause when game is inactive

threatVectors: Cross‑site scripting via malicious HTML modification
mitigations: Include a strict Content‑Security‑Policy meta tag (default-src 'self')
Validate puzzle integrity on load (checksum or length check)
Avoid eval and dynamic script injection; keep all code static

adrs.title: Single‑file offline architecture
adrs.context: Addresses NFR-01 (single HTML file) and FR-01 (load puzzles) by embedding all code, assets, and puzzle data in one file
adrs.decision: All JavaScript, CSS, and puzzle JSON are inlined; no external network requests are performed
adrs.consequences: Ensures offline compliance and simple deployment but requires full rebuild to update puzzles or UI

adrs.title: Responsive UI using native CSS
adrs.context: Addresses NFR-02 (responsive design) and FR-05/FR-06 (highlighting) without external UI libraries
adrs.decision: Use CSS Grid/Flexbox and media queries; visual states handled via CSS classes
adrs.consequences: Small footprint and offline‑first, but manual handling of cross‑browser quirks may be needed

adrs.title: Undo/Redo via command stack
adrs.decision: Maintain a stack of move objects with apply() and revert() methods; undo pops and reverts, redo reapplies
adrs.consequences: Adds memory proportional to move count; provides deterministic state rollback and replay

gaps: No quantitative performance targets (e.g., max frame time) were provided in the contract
